en: "You are the sons of the LORD your God. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness on your foreheads for the dead.
zh: “你們是耶和華你們的 神的兒女，不可為了死人割傷自己的身體，也不可使你們的額上光禿；

en: For you are a people holy to the LORD your God, and the LORD has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession, out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
zh: 因為你是屬於耶和華你的 神的聖潔子民；耶和華從地上的萬民中揀選了你們，特作他自己的子民。

en: "You shall not eat any abomination.
zh: “可厭惡之物，你都不可吃。

en: These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
zh: 你們可以吃的牲畜是以下這些：牛、綿羊、山羊、

en: the deer, the gazelle, the roebuck, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope, and the mountain sheep.
zh: 鹿、羚羊、赤鹿、野山羊、麋鹿、野羊、野鹿。

en: Every animal that parts the hoof and has the hoof cloven in two and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.
zh: 在走獸中，分蹄成兩蹄趾，又反芻的走獸，你們都可以吃。

en: Yet of those that chew the cud or have the hoof cloven you shall not eat these: the camel, the hare, and the rock badger, because they chew the cud but do not part the hoof, are unclean for you.
zh: 但是那些反芻或分蹄的走獸中，你們不可吃的有：駱駝、兔子和石貛，因為牠們反芻卻不分蹄，就對你們不潔淨。

en: And the pig, because it parts the hoof but does not chew the cud, is unclean for you. Their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch.
zh: 至於豬，因為牠分蹄卻不反芻，就對你們不潔淨。牠們的肉，你們不可吃；牠們的屍體，你們不可觸摸。

en: "Of all that are in the waters you may eat these: whatever has fins and scales you may eat.
zh: “在水中，你們可以吃的，有以下這些：有鰭有鱗的，你們都可以吃。

en: And whatever does not have fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean for you.
zh: 沒有鰭沒有鱗的，你們都不可吃；這是對你們不潔淨的。

en: "You may eat all clean birds.
zh: “潔淨的鳥類，你們都可以吃。

en: But these are the ones that you shall not eat: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
zh: 你們不可吃的鳥類有以下這些：鷹、狗頭鷹、紅頭鷹、

en: the kite, the falcon of any kind;
zh: 鳶、隼、黑隼，以及這一類的鳥；

en: every raven of any kind;
zh: 烏鴉和這一類的鳥；

en: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;
zh: 駝鳥、夜鷹、海鷹、雀鷹，以及這一類的鳥；

en: the little owl and the short-eared owl, the barn owl
zh: 小貓頭鷹、貓頭鷹、角鴟、

en: and the tawny owl, the carrion vulture and the cormorant,
zh: 小梟、禿鵰、魚鷹、

en: the stork, the heron of any kind; the hoopoe and the bat.
zh: 鸛、鷺，以及這一類的鳥；戴勝和蝙蝠。

en: And all winged insects are unclean for you; they shall not be eaten.
zh: 有翅膀的昆蟲，對你們都不潔淨，你們都不可吃。

en: All clean winged things you may eat.
zh: 潔淨的鳥類，你們都可以吃。

en: "You shall not eat anything that has died naturally. You may give it to the sojourner who is within your towns, that he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner. For you are a people holy to the LORD your God. "You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
zh: “自死的動物，你們都不可吃；可以送給你城裡的寄居者吃，或是賣給外族人吃，因為你是歸耶和華為聖的子民；不可用山羊羔母的奶煮山羊羔。

en: "You shall tithe all the yield of your seed that comes from the field year by year.
zh: “你要把你撒種所產的，就是田地每年所出的，獻上十分之一。

en: And before the LORD your God, in the place that he will choose, to make his name dwell there, you shall eat the tithe of your grain, of your wine, and of your oil, and the firstborn of your herd and flock, that you may learn to fear the LORD your God always.
zh: 又要把你的五穀、新酒和新油的十分之一，以及你牛群羊群中頭生的，在耶和華你的 神面前吃，就是在他選擇作他名的居所的地方吃，好使你可以學習常常敬畏耶和華你的 神。

en: And if the way is too long for you, so that you are not able to carry the tithe, when the LORD your God blesses you, because the place is too far from you, which the LORD your God chooses, to set his name there,
zh: 耶和華你的 神賜福與你的時候，耶和華你的 神選擇要立為他名的地方，如果離你太遠，那路又太長，使你不能把供物帶到那裡去；

en: then you shall turn it into money and bind up the money in your hand and go to the place that the LORD your God chooses
zh: 你就可以換成銀子，把銀子拿在手裡，到耶和華你的 神選擇的地方去，

en: and spend the money for whatever you desire- oxen or sheep or wine or strong drink, whatever your appetite craves. And you shall eat there before the LORD your God and rejoice, you and your household.
zh: 你可以用這銀子隨意買牛羊、清酒和烈酒；你心想要的，都可以買；在那裡，你和你的家人，都要在耶和華你的 神面前吃喝歡樂。

en: And you shall not neglect the Levite who is within your towns, for he has no portion or inheritance with you.
zh: 住在你城裡的利未人，你不可丟棄他，因為他在你們中間無分無業。

en: "At the end of every three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in the same year and lay it up within your towns.
zh: “每三年之末，你要把那一年的全部出產的十分之一取出來，積存在你的城裡；

en: And the Levite, because he has no portion or inheritance with you, and the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow, who are within your towns, shall come and eat and be filled, that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands that you do.
zh: 這樣，利未人 ，以及在你城裡的寄居者和孤兒寡婦，就可以來，吃得飽足；好叫耶和華你的 神在你手所作的一切事上，賜福給你。”
